methodology.name: Self-Refine
methodology.core_concept: An iterative self-refinement algorithm where a language model refines its own output without additional training.

key_steps.description: The initial output generated by the model is passed back to the same model to generate feedback.
The generated feedback is passed back to the same model to refine the previously generated output.

methodology.process: The feedback and refine steps are repeated iteratively, either for a set number of iterations or until the model determines no further improvement is possible.

implementation_details.prompting_technique: Few-shot prompting is used to guide the model in generating feedback and performing refinement.
implementation_details.model_used: The method is instantiated with a language model such as GPT-3.5.
implementation_details.key_characteristic: It uses the same underlying language model for both feedback generation and refinement, without requiring human assistance or additional training.

problem_addressed.description: Modern LLMs can generate coherent outputs but often fall short on tasks with intricate requirements or hard-to-define goals, producing outputs that could benefit from iterative refinement.
problem_addressed.limitations_of_previous_approaches: Existing iterative refinement methods typically require domain-specific data, large training sets, or expensive human annotations, which may not be feasible.

evaluation.key_results: The Self-Refine method outperforms direct generation from strong LLMs like GPT-3.5.
